db.db_description: This database is a full implementation of the OHDSI OMOP Common Data Model (CDM) version 5.3.1, used to standardize observational patient-level health data for analytics and research. Per its cdm_source record, it holds a Synthea-generated synthetic patient population. The schema includes the complete set of OMOP CDM components: standardized clinical event tables (person, observation_period, visit_occurrence, condition_occurrence, procedure_occurrence, drug_exposure, measurement, observation, device_exposure, specimen, death, note), derived "era" tables (condition_era, drug_era, dose_era), health economics tables (cost, payer_plan_period), the standardized vocabulary/terminology tables (concept, concept_relationship, concept_ancestor, concept_synonym, concept_class, vocabulary, domain, relationship, drug_strength, source_to_concept_map), and metadata/derived-element tables (cdm_source, metadata, cohort_definition, attribute_definition, fact_relationship, location, care_site, provider). In practice the instance is partially populated: it contains 2,694 persons with associated visits (1,037 inpatient encounters), 65,719 condition eras, 52,508 drug eras, ~67,707 drug exposures, 37,409 procedures, 1,477 observations, and measurement records, all backed by the standard vocabularies (RxNorm, SNOMED, LOINC, etc.). Many optional tables — cost, payer/insurance coverage, devices, specimens, deaths, notes/NLP, locations, care sites, providers, and several vocabulary/cohort metadata tables — are empty, which is typical of a synthetic, vocabulary-anchored OMOP CDM dataset focused on core clinical events.

concept.reasoning: Column names (concept_id, concept_name, domain_id, vocabulary_id, concept_class_id, standard_concept, concept_code, valid_start_date, valid_end_date, invalid_reason) exactly match the OMOP CDM CONCEPT table. Sample values confirm it: vocabularies RxNorm/SNOMED/LOINC/CVX, domains Drug/Condition/Measurement, standard_concept 'S', sentinel dates 1970-01-01 and 2099-12-31. This is the OHDSI/OMOP standardized vocabulary concept table.

columns.name: valid_end_date

columns.name: invalid_reason

concept_ancestor.reasoning: Table name and column names exactly match the OMOP CDM CONCEPT_ANCESTOR table. It stores hierarchical (transitive closure) relationships between concepts: each row links an ancestor concept to a descendant concept along with the min/max levels of separation in the hierarchy. Both ancestor_concept_id and descendant_concept_id reference concept.concept_id (the FK to descendant is recovered; ancestor logically references the same). The min_levels_of_separation values starting at 0 confirm self-references (a concept is its own ancestor at level 0), consistent with OMOP semantics. Low distinct_ratio of the separation columns (small integer ranges 0-14) matches level-counting fields.
concept_ancestor.table_description: OMOP CDM CONCEPT_ANCESTOR table: a transitive-closure hierarchy table where each row records that one concept is an ancestor of another, including the minimum and maximum number of hierarchical levels separating them.

columns.name: ancestor_concept_id
columns.description: Reference to concept.concept_id for the higher-level (ancestor) concept in a hierarchical relationship; part of the composite key identifying the ancestor-descendant pair.

columns.name: descendant_concept_id
columns.description: Foreign key to concept.concept_id for the lower-level (descendant) concept subsumed by the ancestor concept.

columns.name: min_levels_of_separation
columns.description: Minimum number of hierarchy levels between the ancestor and descendant concept; 0 indicates a concept's relationship to itself.

columns.name: max_levels_of_separation
columns.description: Maximum number of hierarchy levels between the ancestor and descendant concept along the longest path.

This table classifies clinical concepts into classes within vocabularies. The concept_class_concept_id is a self-referencing link to the CONCEPT table that describes the concept class itself. The table is empty (row_count 0), so descriptions are based on naming/standard conventions, lowering confidence.

concept_relationship.reasoning: This is the OMOP CDM (Common Data Model) standardized vocabulary table 'concept_relationship'. Column names match the OMOP spec exactly: concept_id_1, concept_id_2, relationship_id, valid_start_date, valid_end_date, invalid_reason. The relationship_id examples ('Maps to', 'Mapped from', 'Is a', 'Subsumes') are canonical OMOP relationship codes. concept_id_1/2 are large integers referencing the concept table. valid_end_date is all 2099-12-31 (open/active). invalid_reason is all null (active relationships).
concept_relationship.table_description: An OMOP Common Data Model 'concept_relationship' table that records directional relationships between pairs of standardized vocabulary concepts (e.g., mapping and hierarchical links). Each row links concept_id_1 to concept_id_2 via a named relationship type valid over a date range.

columns.name: valid_start_date

columns.name: valid_end_date

columns.name: invalid_reason
